discoverability.competitive_assessment: Day 10 of steady state. Both sites remain absent from all search indices. GSC verification is 5 days old (2026-04-12). Google crawl-to-index typically takes 1-2 weeks post verification + sitemap submission, so we are still inside the normal window; expect first indexing between 2026-04-19 and 2026-04-26 if the human submits the sitemap via Search Console. Dome machine-readable layer remains frozen at 2026-04-08 snapshot — ai_manifest.json V51.1 (69 confirmed, 94.5%), sitemap 22 URLs lastmod 2026-04-08, llms.txt unchanged. That 9-day freeze on the dome's AI-discoverability files is itself notable: despite the continuing bot cadence updating status_history.json, the author-authored (or author-authorized) strategic discoverability layer has not advanced. Our repo metadata SEO advantage over the dome repo continues to widen in proportional terms — every day without the dome author adding a description/topics/homepage is a day of extra advantage when indexing begins.

human_decisions_needed.question: Bing Webmaster Tools / IndexNow setup
human_decisions_needed.context: Only remaining indexing infrastructure task from SOCIAL-NOTE-002. No BingSiteAuth.xml in docs/, no msvalidate meta tag, no IndexNow key file detected. Counterargument: Google dominates search (~90% US share) and GSC is already verified, so Bing adds marginal value (Bing + DuckDuckGo ~5–8% combined); we have been in a search vacuum for 10+ days on both engines, so the real blocker is crawl-to-index latency, not engine coverage. Recommendation: still worth ~15 minutes of human effort because DuckDuckGo uses Bing and IndexNow can push pages proactively (unlike Google, which is pull-only). No urgency — revisit if we are still unindexed on Bing after 3 weeks post-GSC.

no_action_gaps.reasoning: Our site is a single-page review with progressive disclosure. More sitemap URLs would require more pages, which we do not need — our structure is deliberate. A sitemap with 2 genuine URLs is cleaner than one padded with fragments. Unchanged assessment.

queries.notes: Day 10. 'ECM' keyword space dominated by enterprise content management, extracellular matrix biology, and engine control modules. 'Ovoid cavity cosmological model' as a full phrase still returns zero exact matches — the phrase is unique in search but unindexed.

Day 10. Neither site indexed. Expected for our site since GSC verification (2026-04-12) was only 5 days ago and sitemap submission is pending. Expected for dome site since they have no Search Console account. First indexing window: 2026-04-19 to 2026-04-26 for us.

Generic biblical firmament content dominates. john09289 and jesusrules27163822 remain invisible in search. Author identity anchor is only useful once the sites are indexed; until then it returns zero attribution results.
Parallel frameworks continue to own this keyword neighborhood. ANIMA Framework and Journal of Geocentric Cosmology remain the dominant players; ECM absent from its own natural keywords.

Dome's signature numerical parameter (disc radius 20,015 km = π × Earth's radius) returns zero ECM-specific results. This is arguably the single most distinctive technical string the dome publishes, yet it is invisible in search. Either way, when the dome becomes indexed this phrase will surface us too — our kill-shot analysis uses the exact figure.
Finsler geometry + flat-earth remains a genuinely unique ECM signature. No other framework uses it. Zero search results. When indexed this will be a near-perfect anchor for attribution.

search_monitoring.trend_summary: Day 10 of search vacuum. No search-index movement for either site. The five rebrand-resistant anchors we identified (author identity john09289, repo name dome-model-review, signature parameter disc radius 20,015 km, Finsler geometry + flat-earth unique combination, aetheric refraction index terminology) all return zero ECM-specific results — meaning the dome author has not managed organic discovery via any of these and we have the same problem. The structural difference: when indexing begins, our repo metadata (description, homepage URL, 6 topics) gives us a clean first-impression; the dome repo has no metadata so any inbound visitor from a code-search query sees an anonymous repo. Competitor frameworks (ANIMA, Journal of Geocentric Cosmology, Gravitic Alchemy) continue to saturate the 'firmament'/'aether' keyword neighborhood — those are indexed because they are WordPress sites with established backlinks. Our and the dome's github.io paths need the crawl-invitation path (GSC + Bing Webmaster) to escape the zero-index state.
